components.name: magnesium sulfate (MgSO4)
components.role: salt (Mg, sulfate) - replaces (NH4)2SO4 in N2-fixing medium
components.evidence: In the case of no-NH4+ minimal medium, the 1 g/liter (NH4)2SO4 was replaced with 0.2 g/liter MgSO4 to achieve similar molarity.

components.name: iron(II) sulfate heptahydrate (FeSO4.7H2O)
components.evidence: 2.5 mg/liter FeSO4.7 H2O ... One thousand-fold solutions of FeSO4 (2.5 g/liter)

components.name: dinitrogen gas (N2)
components.role: nitrogen source (sole N source, fixed via nitrogenase)
components.evidence: For N2-fixing conditions, no NH4+ was added to the medium and the only available nitrogen source was N2 gas (>90% N2 in the anaerobic chamber).

components.name: carbon dioxide (CO2)
components.evidence: The atmosphere in the anaerobic chamber was composed of 2 to 4% H2, 5% CO2, and the remaining percentage N2.

components.name: hydrogen gas (H2)
components.evidence: The atmosphere in the anaerobic chamber was composed of 2 to 4% H2, 5% CO2, and the remaining percentage N2. Oxygen levels were kept below 100 ppm.

media.base_medium: Chemically defined Zymomonas mobilis minimal medium (fully specified in main text; no cited base medium)

sources_chased.what: Medium preparation + Culture conditions sections of the paper itself
sources_chased.how: Read corpus_path JATS XML (Materials and Methods, sections s3-1, s3-2, s3-3)
added: N2 gas as sole N source
anaerobic-chamber gas phase (5% CO2, 2-4% H2, balance N2, O2<100 ppm)

missing_before: MgSO4 (anhydrous, the (NH4)2SO4 substitute providing extra Mg/sulfate)
media.notes: This is a fully chemically-defined minimal medium spelled out completely in the paper's Materials and Methods; there is no cited base medium, no named trace/vitamin stock to expand, and the only vitamin is calcium pantothenate. The N2-fixing variant is identical to the ammonia-replete medium except the 1 g/L (NH4)2SO4 is replaced by 0.2 g/L anhydrous MgSO4 and nitrogen comes solely from N2 gas. Ion-level exchange components in the target were already complete (Ca, Cl, Fe, glucose, K, Mg, Mo, Na, Pi, pantothenate, sulfate; correctly no ammonium).

components.name: ammonium (added supplement)
components.evidence: used to inoculate subsequent cultures which contained either 15 mM NH4+ or no NH4+

components.name: carbon dioxide (CO2)
components.evidence: The atmosphere in the anaerobic chamber was composed of 2 to 4% H2, 5% CO2, and the remaining percentage N2.

components.name: hydrogen gas (H2)
components.evidence: 2 to 4% H2, 5% CO2, and the remaining percentage N2. Oxygen levels were kept below 100 ppm.

media.base_medium: Chemically defined Zymomonas mobilis minimal medium (fully specified in main text; no cited base medium)

sources_chased.what: Medium preparation + Culture conditions sections of the paper itself
sources_chased.how: Read corpus_path JATS XML (Materials and Methods, sections s3-1, s3-2)

media.notes: Ammonia-replete variant of the same fully-defined Z. mobilis ZM4 minimal medium. Recipe verbatim in the target's verification_evidence and confirmed against Materials and Methods. Contains ammonium sulfate as base N plus subcultures brought to 15 mM NH4+ (replete). Only vitamin is calcium pantothenate; only trace metals are Fe and Mo; no cited base medium or named stock mix to expand. All ion-level exchange components in the target were already present.
